text: The parking assistance system shall support three operational modes: Automatic Parking Assist (APA), Remote Parking Assist (RPA), and Summon function. Mode selection shall be performed exclusively by the driver via the HMI. All modes operate under SAE Level 2 automation.

The system architecture shall comply with ISO 26262 for functional safety and ISO 21448 (SOTIF) for safety of the intended functionality. A hazard analysis and risk assessment (HARA) shall be conducted for each parking mode defined in SYS-001.

The Automatic Parking Assist function shall detect and classify parking spaces (parallel, perpendicular, and angled slots) using a fusion of ultrasonic sensors and the 360-degree surround-view camera system. Minimum detectable slot width shall be 2.2 m for perpendicular and 5.5 m for parallel parking.

The trajectory planning module of APA-001 shall compute a collision-free parking path within 500 ms of slot confirmation. The path shall be replanned continuously at 50 ms intervals to account for dynamic obstacles detected by the obstacle detection subsystem as defined in PER-003.

The APA electronic control unit (ECU) shall implement ASIL-B integrity level as required by ISO 26262 Part 5 for steer-by-wire actuator commands. All safety-relevant signals between the parking ECU and the steering ECU shall use E2E protection profile P02.

During active APA maneuver, the driver shall maintain continuous visual monitoring of the vehicle surroundings and must be prepared to press the brake pedal or release the control button at any time to immediately abort the maneuver. APA shall not conflict with any concurrent active ADAS feature as defined in SYS-001.

The Remote Parking Assist function shall enable the driver to maneuver the vehicle into or out of a parking space from outside the vehicle via a dedicated smartphone application or key fob. The driver must be within 6 metres line-of-sight of the vehicle during the entire maneuver.

The RPA communication channel between the smartphone application and the vehicle gateway ECU shall use Bluetooth Low Energy 5.2 with AES-128 encryption. End-to-end latency shall not exceed 100 ms under nominal channel conditions to satisfy the real-time control requirement of RPA-001.

The RPA system shall achieve ASIL-D decomposition for the dead-man switch (continuous button press) monitoring function, as the loss of this function could lead to uncontrolled vehicle movement with no driver inside. This supersedes the ASIL-B allocation of APA-003 for shared actuator paths.

The Summon function shall autonomously navigate the vehicle from a parking space to a pre-defined pick-up zone designated by the driver via the connected vehicle app. The maximum travel distance for summon shall not exceed 50 m and vehicle speed shall be limited to 5 km/h. Summon depends on the slot classification capability of APA-001.

The Summon function shall be disabled automatically if the ultrasonic sensor self-test defined in SEN-001 reports a fault, if GPS positioning error exceeds 0.5 m RMS, or if the vehicle-to-cloud heartbeat latency exceeds 200 ms. All three conditions shall trigger a safe state transition and driver notification via the HMI.

The ultrasonic sensor array shall consist of 12 sensors (4 front, 4 rear, 2 front-corner, 2 rear-corner) operating at 48 kHz. Each sensor shall perform a self-test at system startup and every 500 ms during active parking modes. Detection range shall be 0.2 m to 5.5 m with a lateral resolution of +/-0.1 m. SEN-001 is a precondition for APA-001.

The 360-degree surround-view camera system shall comprise four fisheye cameras (front, rear, left, right) with a minimum resolution of 1280x960 pixels at 30 fps. The image stitching pipeline shall produce a calibrated bird-eye view within 50 ms and deliver it to the APA-001 slot detection module and the SUM-001 navigation pipeline.

The obstacle detection subsystem shall identify static and dynamic obstacles within the parking maneuver envelope using sensor fusion of SEN-001 ultrasonic data and SEN-002 camera data. A detected obstacle shall trigger a vehicle stop within 150 ms. False positive rate shall be below 0.1% under nominal lighting and weather conditions.

The parking HMI shall provide real-time visual feedback of the surround-view camera image, detected parking slot boundaries, planned trajectory, and active sensor status during all modes of SYS-001. Alerts for takeover requests or system faults shall be displayed within 200 ms and accompanied by an auditory signal of at least 65 dB.

In the event of a critical safety fault (ASIL-B or higher as classified in SYS-002), the HMI defined in HMI-001 shall display a persistent red warning and initiate a controlled stop of the active parking maneuver. The driver shall be required to acknowledge the fault before any parking mode from SYS-001 can be re-activated.

Verification of APA-001, RPA-001, and SUM-001 functional requirements shall be conducted using model-in-the-loop (MIL) simulation covering at least 500 representative parking scenarios including edge cases for sensor degradation, adverse weather, and mixed pedestrian and vehicle environments. Pass criteria: zero safety violations, slot detection accuracy above 98%.

The functional safety case for RPA-003 ASIL-D decomposition shall be documented as a safety case argument in accordance with ISO 26262 Part 2. The argument shall demonstrate independence between the two ASIL-B channels and provide quantitative evidence of the achieved probability of failure per hour below 10^-8.
